146.reason: // Error: The condition should be (content.charAt(i)!='*' || content.charAt(i+1)!='/') to correctly detect the end of a multi-line comment "*/". The current condition checks for "/" and "*" in the wrong order, causing the loop to skip over the closing "*/" and potentially miss subsequent tokens.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  // Error: The condition should be (content.charAt(i)!='*' || content.charAt(i+1)!='/') to correctly detect the end of a multi-line comment "*/". The current condition checks for "/" and "*" in the wrong order, causing the loop to skip over the closing "*/" and potentially miss subsequent tokens.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: The condition for ending multi-line comment is incorrect. It should check for "*/" (content.charAt(i)=='*' && content.charAt(i+1)=='/'), but the current condition checks for "/*" (content.charAt(i)=='/' && content.charAt(i+1)=='*'), which will never match the end of the comment, causing the loop to go out of bounds or skip the closing "*/".]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: The loop condition incorrectly checks for "/*" instead of "*/", and does not guard against out-of-bounds access when i+1 equals content.length(), causing potential ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException. Also, the loop may not properly skip the closing "*/".]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [IndexOutOfBoundsException: Inside the loop, when i is at the last character, content.charAt(i+1) throws an exception.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The loop condition is incorrect: it stops when it sees '/' followed by '*', but it should stop when it sees '*' followed by '/'. This causes incorrect handling of multi-line comments and may lead to missing tokens or infinite loops.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The loop condition is incorrect: it should check for '*' followed by '/', not '/' followed by '*'. This causes the lexer to skip until it finds "/*" again instead of "*/", leading to incorrect comment handling and premature termination of tokenization.]

52.reason: The loop condition checks for '*' followed by '/' to end the comment, but fails to increment lineNum when encountering newlines inside the comment. This causes incorrect line number tracking for tokens appearing after multi-line comments. Additionally, if the comment is at the end of file without proper termination, it might cause IndexOutOfBoundsException due to content.charAt(i+1) check without boundary check for i+1.
